turns.name: AMY CONEY BARRETT
turns.text: I also have the Court’s opinion in Michelle O’Connor-Ratcliff versus Christopher Garnier. This case presents the same issue as Lindke. Michelle O’Connor-Ratcliff and T. J. Zane were members of the Poway United School District Board of Trustees. In addition to their personal Facebook pages, they operated public pages where they posted about school board related issues. They blocked Christopher and Kimberly Garnier after the Garniers posted lengthy and repetitive comments on their pages and the Garniers sued under Section 93. The District Court allowed their case to proceed based on its conclusion that the trustees acted under color of state law. The Ninth Circuit affirmed. It said that given the official appearance and content of the trustees’ pages, there is a close nexus between their social media activity and their official positions. The Ninth Circuit’s approach is different from the one we have elaborated in Lindke. We vacate the judgment below and remand for further proceedings consistent with this opinion, and again this opinion is unanimous.
